name: Ye are the temple of God
notes: In 2 Corinthians 6:16-18 Paul weaves together several Old Testament texts to ground his appeal that believers, as the temple of the living God, be separate from idols. The shared core is the covenant promise of God's dwelling presence: from Leviticus 26:12 (echoed in Ezekiel 37:27) he draws "I will be their God, and they shall be my people"; from Isaiah 52:11 the summons to come out and "touch not the unclean thing"; and from 2 Samuel 7:14 (with the sons-and-daughters breadth of Isaiah 43:6) the promise "I will be a Father unto you." Paul does not quote any single source word for word but gathers their language into one unified declaration, so the wording is adapted as the Spirit knits the passages together. Each source keeps its own vantage: Leviticus and Ezekiel speak of God walking among His covenant people, Isaiah 52 of priestly separation in bearing the vessels of the LORD, and 2 Samuel 7 of the Davidic father-son promise widened in Isaiah 43 to sons and daughters from afar. The differences in exact phrasing between the sources and Paul's rendering are left as a matter for further study.

links.label: I will be their God and they shall be my people
I will be ... father ... he shall be my son
